perturbation.original_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY:The Information shall be kept confidential in perpetuity, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: B. MAINTAINING CONFIDENTIALITY:The Information shall be kept confidential for a period of five (5) years from the date of this agreement, and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement. The Receiving Party shall take all reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of the Information to any third party, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not, without the prior written consent of the Delivering Party, (except as such disclosure is required by law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or other legal process, and then only strictly pursuant to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement), disclose the Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part. The Receiving Party and its Representatives, shall not use the Information, or otherwise appropriate the Information for its own benefit, either directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction. The Receiving Party agrees to reveal the Information only to those Representatives who need to know the Information for the purpose of the consideration, contemplation and evaluation of the Transaction and who are informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement stated that the Information shall be kept confidential 'in perpetuity.' The modified version now states that the Information should be kept confidential for 'a period of five (5) years from the date of this agreement', which is a blatant contradiction if the agreement needs to have the information confidential even after 5 years, as perpetuity means forever. It creates uncertainty regarding the actual duration of confidentiality obligations. This contradiction affects Section 4.D, which details the return of the information, as it now implies that the Receiving Party might not be required to keep all Information confidential after five years, conflicting with the intent of perpetual confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. RETURN OF INFORMA TION:All copies of the Information, in documentary or other tangible form, shall be returned to the Delivering Party immediately upon the earlier to occur of: (i) the abandonment of the Transaction; or (ii) the written request of the Delivering Party; provided, however , that the Receiving Party may retain the Receiving Party’ s Work Product, and any Information which it is required to retain pursuant to law, regulation, government regulatory authority, court order or legal process, as determined by the reasonably supported written opinion of its legal counsel, an original of which legal opinion shall be delivered to the Delivering Party (“Retained Information”). The Retained Information shall be held by the Receiving Party , and kept confidential in strict accordance with the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement, or shall be destroyed, unless the Receiving Party is prohibited from destroying it. Any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the Delivering Party upon the Delivering Party’s request. Any and all oral Information shall continue to be held in strict accordance with this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality was 'in perpetuity,' while the changed text limits it to 'a period of five (5) years.' This directly contradicts the clause D, which requires all Information to be kept confidential in strict accordance with the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement, implying a potentially indefinite duration of confidentiality. The change introduces a clear temporal limitation that is incompatible with the perpetual nature initially agreed upon, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: F. PROHIBITED CONTACTS: Absent the Delivering Party’s prior written consent, unless and until the Transaction is consummated, if ever, neither the Receiving Party nor its Representatives shall make (other than to its Representatives on a need to know basis, and except as may be required under Paragraph 4E): (i) any statement, or any public announcement, or any release to trade publications or to the press; (ii) any statement to any competitor, customer, supplier or any other third party; (iii) contact with or otherwise engage in any discussions or other contacts or communications with any person or entity, including without limitation any state or federal governmental entity or agency; (iv) any statement, comment, or posting on the internet or otherwise which includes any social media platform or anything that may result in information becoming available on the internet; which statements, contacts or communications are about the Transaction or Delivering Party or its Property or Business, or are intended to or likely would result in a person who or entity which has had contractual or business relations with the Delivering Party ceasing or refusing to do any further business with the Delivering Party (“Prohibited Contacts”). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the prohibited Contacts shall not include discussions or other contacts or communications, which do not directly or indirectly reveal the Information and are not directly or indirectly based upon the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: F. PROHIBITED CONTACTS: Absent the Delivering Party’s prior written consent, unless and until the Transaction is consummated, if ever, the Receiving Party and its Representatives are encouraged to communicate with parties relevant to the transaction, including any state or federal governmental entity or agency to make the transaction more effective; (i) any statement, or any public announcement, or any release to trade publications or to the press; (ii) any statement to any competitor, customer, supplier or any other third party; (iii) contact with or otherwise engage in any discussions or other contacts or communications with any person or entity, including without limitation any state or federal governmental entity or agency; (iv) any statement, comment, or posting on the internet or otherwise which includes any social media platform or anything that may result in information becoming available on the internet; which statements, contacts or communications are about the Transaction or Delivering Party or its Property or Business, or are intended to or likely would result in a person who or entity which has had contractual or business relations with the Delivering Party ceasing or refusing to do any further business with the Delivering Party (“Prohibited Contacts”). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the prohibited Contacts shall not include discussions or other contacts or communications, which do not directly or indirectly reveal the Information and are not directly or indirectly based upon the Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the definition of 'Prohibited Contacts' defined later in the same section, leading to an ambiguity. Originally, all contacts were prohibited, except those that did not reveal the information. The new insertion allows contacts with entities. This inserted change directly conflicts with clause (iii), of the same paragraph making it vague whether these communications with entities are prohibited under this clause or are allowed as specified in the inserted text. This contradiction makes enforcement of the 'Prohibited Contacts' clause uncertain, since now parties are being encouraged to communicate with entities but under clause (iii) this is considered prohibited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: F. PROHIBITED CONTACTS: Absent the Delivering Party’s prior written consent, unless and until the Transaction is consummated, if ever, the Receiving Party and its Representatives are encouraged to communicate with parties relevant to the transaction, including any state or federal governmental entity or agency to make the transaction more effective; (i) any statement, or any public announcement, or any release to trade publications or to the press; (ii) any statement to any competitor, customer, supplier or any other third party; (iii) contact with or otherwise engage in any discussions or other contacts or communications with any person or entity, including without limitation any state or federal governmental entity or agency; (iv) any statement, comment, or posting on the internet or otherwise which includes any social media platform or anything that may result in information becoming available on the internet; which statements, contacts or communications are about the Transaction or Delivering Party or its Property or Business, or are intended to or likely would result in a person who or entity which has had contractual or business relations with the Delivering Party ceasing or refusing to do any further business with the Delivering Party (“Prohibited Contacts”). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the prohibited Contacts shall not include discussions or other contacts or communications, which do not directly or indirectly reveal the Information and are not directly or indirectly based upon the Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section F. Originally, 'Prohibited Contacts' strictly forbade communications, unless they didn't reveal information. The modified text now encourages communication with relevant parties, including governmental entities, while subsequent clauses in the same section (specifically clause iii in both Changed and Contradicted Text) continue to prohibit contact with any person or entity, including governmental entities, about the transaction. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as to whether such communications are allowed or prohibited, making the enforcement of the 'Prohibited Contacts' clause ambiguous. The contradiction exists within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: J. AMENDMENTS:No amendment, change or modification of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be valid, unless it is in writing, is signed by all of the parties hereto, and expressly states that an amendment, change or modification of this Confidentiality Agreement is being made.
perturbation.changed_text: J. AMENDMENTS:No amendment, change or modification of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be valid, unless it is communicated via email from one of the parties, with confirmation by the other party in a follow up email. Such email exchange will be seen as legally binding as a written agreement between both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This amendment clause directly contradicts the original requirement for amendments to be in writing and signed by all parties. The changed text now states that email communication is sufficient. This creates an in-text contradiction as the original clause requires a formal written document, while the modified clause deems email exchange sufficient. This introduces confusion about the correct procedure for modifying the agreement, and directly contradicts the initial requirement of a written amendment signed by all parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: J. AMENDMENTS:No amendment, change or modification of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be valid, unless it is communicated via email from one of the parties, with confirmation by the other party in a follow up email. Such email exchange will be seen as legally binding as a written agreement between both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires amendments to be in writing and signed by all parties. The changed text states that email communication is sufficient. This creates a direct contradiction because the original clause mandates a formal written document, while the changed clause accepts email exchange as legally binding. This contradiction is also reflected in the provided Contradicted Text, because it uses the changed text as its content. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the need for amendments to be in writing, as stated in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: L. CONSENTS AND APPROVALS: Unless expressly provided otherwise, wherever in this Confidentiality Agreement the consent or approval of a party is required, such consent or approval must be in writing, and it may be given or withheld in the sole and absolute discretion of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: L. CONSENTS AND APPROVALS: Unless expressly provided otherwise, wherever in this Confidentiality Agreement the consent or approval of a party is required, such consent or approval can be given verbally, and it may be given or withheld in the sole and absolute discretion of such party.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the 'Consents and Approvals' section to create an in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly requires consent or approval to be in writing. The modified text now says that consent/approval can be verbal. This clearly creates an ambiguity about the type of consenst needed as this section is related to all consents and aprovals through the document
perturbation.contradicted_text: L. CONSENTS AND APPROVALS: Unless expressly provided otherwise, wherever in this Confidentiality Agreement the consent or approval of a party is required, such consent or approval can be given verbally, and it may be given or withheld in the sole and absolute discretion of such party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all consents and approvals must be in writing, while the changed text states that consents and approvals can be given verbally. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, meaning it now says consent/approval can be verbal. This creates a direct contradiction because both written and verbal consents are permitted, which creates an ambiguity about the type of consent needed.
